title: Balangir Extends School and College Holiday Till April 25 Amid Heatwave - pragativadi.com
search_term: heatwave (Angul OR Balangir OR Balasore OR Bargarh OR Bhadrak OR Boudh OR Cuttack OR Deogarh OR Dhenkanal OR Gajapati OR Ganjam OR Jagatsinghapur OR Jajpur OR Jharsuguda OR Kalahandi OR Kandhamal OR Kendrapara OR "Kendujhar (Keonjhar)" OR Khordha OR Koraput OR Malkangiri OR Mayurbhanj OR Nabarangpur OR Nayagarh OR Nuapada OR Puri OR Rayagada OR Sambalpur OR Sonepur OR Sundargarh) Odisha
full_text:
Balangir district administration extended the holiday for schools, colleges, and vocational institutions until April 25 due to the prevailing heatwave.
Earlier, the closure was announced from April 18 to April 21, but District Collector Gaurav Shivaji Islawar confirmed the extension to safeguard students and staff.
All schools, colleges, and Anganwadi centres across the district will remain closed during this period. Authorities clarified that examinations already scheduled will continue as planned, ensuring academic timelines remain intact.
Officials emphasised that the decision reflects the administration’s proactive approach to protecting children and young adults from extreme weather conditions. Parents and teachers welcomed the move, noting that the scorching temperatures had made regular attendance unsafe.
The directive also highlighted the importance of balancing academic responsibilities with health and safety. Education officers assured that necessary adjustments would be made to minimise disruption once institutions reopen.
